title: Labor, slavery, and caste in the Spanish colonial system
description: Practice your historical skills in this quiz on labor, slavery, and caste in the Spanish colonial system
topic_path: Labor, slavery, and caste in the Spanish colonial system

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below.

>"Into this land of meek outcasts there came some Spaniards who immediately behaved like ravening wild beasts . . . killing, terrorizing, afflicting, torturing, and destroying the native peoples, doing all this with the strangest and most varied new methods of cruelty, never seen or heard of before, and to such a degree that this Island of Hispaniola once so populous (having a population that I estimated to be more than three million), has now a population of barely two hundred persons.”

>-Source: Bartolomé de Las Casas, *Brief Account of the Devastation of the Indies*, 1542.

**Which of the following practices did the ideas expressed in the excerpt most directly challenge?** 


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: enslaving indigenous people as laborers in the *encomienda* system
converting indigenous people to Christianity through missionary work
mixed-race relationships between Spaniards and indigenous people
taking indigenous land for colonization by creating unfair treaties

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below. 

>"Into this land of meek outcasts there came some Spaniards who immediately behaved like ravening wild beasts . . . killing, terrorizing, afflicting, torturing, and destroying the native peoples, doing all this with the strangest and most varied new methods of cruelty, never seen or heard of before, and to such a degree that this Island of Hispaniola once so populous (having a population that I estimated to be more than three million), has now a population of barely two hundred persons.”

>-Source: Bartolomé de Las Casas, *Brief Account of the Devastation of the Indies*, 1542.

**Which of the following historical figures most likely would agree with de Las Casas’s account of Spanish colonization?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Moctezuma II
Christopher Columbus
Francisco Pizarro
Hernan Cortés

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below. 

>As we wish the said Indians to be converted to our Holy Catholic Faith, and to be taught its doctrines . . . I command you to compel and oblige the said Indians to deal and associate with the Christians of the said island, to work in their buildings in collecting and mining gold and other metals, and to grow food and supplies for the Christian settlers and inhabitants of the said island . . . . and this they are to do as the free persons they are, and not as slaves; and you are to see to it that the said Indians are well treated . . .
 
>-Source: Queen Isabella to Nicolas de Ovando, Governor of Hispaniola, 1503 

**Which of the following was the most immediate result of the edict excerpted?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the *encomienda* system
the Reconquista
the transatlantic slave trade
the Spanish caste system

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below. 

>As we wish the said Indians to be converted to our Holy Catholic Faith, and to be taught its doctrines . . . I command you to compel and oblige the said Indians to deal and associate with the Christians of the said island, to work in their buildings in collecting and mining gold and other metals, and to grow food and supplies for the Christian settlers and inhabitants of the said island . . . . and this they are to do as the free persons they are, and not as slaves; and you are to see to it that the said Indians are well treated . . .
 
>-Queen Isabella to Nicolas de Ovando, Governor of Hispaniola, 1503 

 ** Which of the following contributed most directly to the enactment of the law in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: European demands for laborers in the New World
indigenous nations’ requests for religious instruction
religious conflict during the Protestant Reformation
epidemic diseases reducing the indigenous population

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage and answer the question below. 

> “By virtue of the said ransom, let ships go there and bring away as many male and female Negroes as possible, newly imported and between the ages of fifteen to eighteen or twenty years. . .  The burden of work of the Indians will be eased and unlimited amounts of gold will be mined. This is the best land in the world for Negroes, women and old men, and it is very rarely that one of these people die.”

>-Source: Alonso de Zuazo, Spanish colonial judge, 1518

**This excerpt best reflects which of the following historical developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: responses to the change in the indigenous populations after exposure to European diseases
debates between the Spanish crown and Spanish religious leaders about the treatment of non-Europeans
indigenous rebellions against Spanish colonialism such as the Pueblo Revolt
conflicts over the goals of Spanish colonization and the use of forced labor

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage and answer the question below. 

> “By virtue of the said ransom, let ships go there and bring away as many male and female Negroes as possible, newly imported and between the ages of fifteen to eighteen or twenty years. . .  The burden of work of the Indians will be eased and unlimited amounts of gold will be mined. This is the best land in the world for Negroes, women and old men, and it is very rarely that one of these people die.”

>-Source: Alonso de Zuazo, Spanish colonial judge, 1518

**Which of the following contributed most to the increasing use of African slave labor instead of Native American labor in colonial Spain?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Enslaved Africans were immune to most diseases spread by the Spanish, while indigenous people were not.
Enslaved Africans had already converted to Christianity, satisfying the Spanish crown.
Enslaved Africans had knowledge of where to find gold and silver mines in the Americas.
Enslaved Africans could cultivate cash crops at a faster rate, satisfying European demands for agricultural products.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below. 

>It is public opinion and knowledge that no end of deception is practiced and a thousand acts of robbery and violence are committed in the course of bartering and carrying off Negroes from their country and bringing them to the Indies and to Spain. . . . Since the Portuguese and Spaniards pay so much for a Negro, they go out to hunt one another without the pretext of a war, as if they were deer; even the very Ethiopians, who are different, being induced to do so by the profit derived. . . . They embark four and five hundred of them in a boat which, sometimes, is not a cargo boat. The very stench is enough to kill most of them, and, indeed, very many die. The wonder is that twenty percent of them are not lost.

>-Source: Fray Tomas de Mercado, Spanish Dominican Friar, 1587

 **Which of the following developments contributed most directly to the conditions described in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: European mines and plantations in the New World
military conflict between West African peoples
lax enforcement of the British Navigation Acts
forced religious conversions during the Reconquista

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below. 

>It is public opinion and knowledge that no end of deception is practiced and a thousand acts of robbery and violence are committed in the course of bartering and carrying off Negroes from their country and bringing them to the Indies and to Spain. . . . Since the Portuguese and Spaniards pay so much for a Negro, they go out to hunt one another without the pretext of a war, as if they were deer; even the very Ethiopians, who are different, being induced to do so by the profit derived. . . . They embark four and five hundred of them in a boat which, sometimes, is not a cargo boat. The very stench is enough to kill most of them, and, indeed, very many die. The wonder is that twenty percent of them are not lost.

>-Source: Fray Tomas de Mercado, Spanish Dominican Friar, 1587

**The excerpt is best understood as a response to which of the following historical developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the Atlantic slave trade
the *encomienda* system
the Pueblo Revolt
the consumer revolution

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the image and answer the question below. 

![Spanish casta painting categorizing mixed-race children](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3a7f81cb7b0402e1baef7d9d4b87f536.png)

*Eighteenth-century Spanish casta painting.* Image credit: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Casta_painting_all.jpg) 

**This painting could best be used as evidence of which of the following historical developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: how Spanish colonization led to a racial hierarchy in the New World based on an individual's percentage of Spanish blood
how Spanish colonization led to the rapid acceptance of interracial families in the New World
how Spanish colonization led to the rejection of racial hierarchy in the New World
how Spanish colonization created a society in the New World which excluded indigenous people

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the image and answer the question below. 

![Spanish casta painting categorizing mixed-race children](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3a7f81cb7b0402e1baef7d9d4b87f536.png)

*Eighteenth-century Spanish casta painting.* Image credit: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Casta_painting_all.jpg) 

**Which of the following most directly led to the circumstances illustrated by the image?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: intermarriage between Europeans, Africans, and indigenous people
military resistance to European invasion by indigenous nations
political alliances between European nations in colonial America
protection of indigenous peoples’ and Africans’ religious practices

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the image and answer the question below. 

![Spanish casta painting categorizing mixed-race children](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3a7f81cb7b0402e1baef7d9d4b87f536.png)

*Eighteenth-century Spanish casta painting.* Image credit: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Casta_painting_all.jpg) 

**This painting was intended to:**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: define individuals’ social status in Spanish North America.
illustrate the effects of European diseases in the New World.
promote Spanish fashions to wealthy colonial consumers.
exclude indigenous and African people from society.

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the image and answer the question below. 

![Spanish casta painting categorizing mixed-race children](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3a7f81cb7b0402e1baef7d9d4b87f536.png)

*Eighteenth-century Spanish casta painting.* Image credit: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Casta_painting_all.jpg) 

**The image most directly reflects which of the following during the Spanish colonial era?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

choices.content: the development of a race-based caste system
the emergence of syncretic religious practices
the decline of the *asiento* system
the enfranchisement of indigenous men and women

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage and answer the question below. 

> “By the middle of the sixteenth century, the Spanish state . . . was creating a political, economic, and institutional framework . . . which consisted of two separate but interrelated politics or ‘republics,’ the Indian and the Spanish, [and] was one of the most distinctive aspects of Spanish colonial rule. It essentially allowed for the political and socioeconomic subordination of the indigenous people at the same time that it granted them a special status as Christian vassals of the Crown of Castile. According to Spanish colonial ideology, the native people’s acceptance of the Catholic faith made them into a spiritually favored and unsullied population with their voluntary subjection to the Castilian monarch earned them rights similar to those enjoyed by natives of Spanish kingdoms.”

> -Source: María Elena Martínez, historian, *Genealogical Fictions: Limpieza de Sangre, Religion, and Gender in Colonial Mexico,* 2008


**Which of the following cultural and social shifts resulted most directly from the trends described in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the incorporation of indigenous people into Spanish society
the creation of Spanish charters for colonies run by indigenous leaders
the spread of smallpox to wipe out the indigenous population
the start of several wars between Europeans to expand colonial territory
